description_long: This is a short wooden fence section composed of four vertical pickets with pointed tops, each attached to a single horizontal rail. The pickets are unevenly spaced and slightly angled, giving the fence a rustic and irregular appearance. The horizontal rail is fastened to the pickets with visible round metal fasteners, likely bolts or screws. The wood has a medium brown finish, suggesting it is either stained or naturally weathered. The overall construction is simple, with no decorative elements or embellishments. This type of fence is commonly used as a boundary marker or decorative barrier in urban parks, gardens, or residential yards.
description: A rustic, short wooden fence section with four pointed pickets attached to a single horizontal rail and visible metal fasteners. The pickets are unevenly spaced and angled, giving it a handmade, irregular look.
description_view_0: Front view showing four pointed wooden pickets attached to a horizontal rail, with the pickets unevenly spaced and angled.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the pickets and rail appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the horizontal rail with four visible round metal fasteners securing the pickets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the alignment of the pickets and rail.
